food_subgroup: Yeast-raised glazed donut with chocolate icing and pistachio topping

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic doughnut food product class; no specific pistachio chocolate donut subclass exists. closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJX covers doughnuts generically; no pistachio chocolate variant exists. closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for pistachio chocolate donut. none assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Dunkin' Donuts pistachio chocolate donut weighing approximately 75 g, as typically served at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Cake-type and yeast-raised doughnuts, plain and glazed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; supplemented with USDA Branded Food entries for chocolate-iced yeast doughnuts
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for a pistachio chocolate donut. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18247 (Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, glazed, enriched) and USDA Branded Food entries for chocolate-iced yeast doughnuts (e.g., FDC ID 2103965). Pistachio topping contributes minor additional fat, protein, and micronutrients; adjustment applied proportionally based on typical 5 g pistachio garnish per 75 g donut. Energy and macros are consistent with published Dunkin' Donuts Singapore nutrition disclosures used as corroborating reference only (not primary source).

gi_source.title: Doughnut, deep-fried — Glycemic Index entry; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 76 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008) for deep-fried doughnuts. No specific GI value exists for a pistachio chocolate donut variant. The analogous estimate uses the published GI for a standard deep-fried glazed doughnut (GI ~76, glucose reference). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 36.9) / 100 = 28.0, rounded to 27.9 using 49.2 g carb per 100 g × 0.75 serving = 36.9 g carb per 75 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (310 mg per 100 g; ~233 mg per 75 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this donut as a single 75 g serving contributes approximately 233 mg sodium (~10% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), and the high sugar and saturated fat content may further contribute to cardiovascular strain when consumed regularly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 76) and high sugar content (22.5 g sugar per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this donut due to its high GI of 76 and glycemic load of approximately 28 per serving, which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, it should be paired with protein or fibre to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (7.2 g per 100 g), trans fat (0.3 g per 100 g), and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this donut as it contains significant saturated fat (~7.2 g/100 g), residual trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g), and high added sugar, all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and increased cardiovascular risk when consumed frequently.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Pistachio Chocolate Donut is a yeast-raised, deep-fried donut topped with a chocolate glaze and garnished with crushed pistachio pieces. It is sold at Dunkin' Donuts outlets in Singapore, including the Funan mall location, and is priced at approximately SGD 3.45. The donut has a soft, airy interior from the yeast-leavening process, a sweet and slightly bitter chocolate icing on the exterior, and a nutty crunch from the pistachio topping. Nutritionally, it is an energy-dense, ultra-processed snack providing approximately 387 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content (~49 g/100 g), moderate fat (~19.5 g/100 g including ~7.2 g saturated fat), and modest protein (~5.8 g/100 g). The high glycemic index (GI ~76) and glycemic load (~28 per 75 g serving) make it a high-glycemic food that can cause rapid blood glucose spikes. It contains gluten, dairy, eggs, and tree nuts (pistachio), making it unsuitable for individuals with these allergies. Clinically, it is of high concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease due to its high GI, saturated fat, and added sugar content. Sodium content (~310 mg/100 g) is moderate and relevant for hypertension management. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary item.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Dunkin Donuts pistachio chocolate donut?
Can diabetics eat a pistachio chocolate donut?
What is the GI of a chocolate donut?
Dunkin Donuts pistachio donut calories Singapore
Is pistachio donut high in sugar?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for a Dunkin' Donuts Singapore pistachio chocolate donut in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Branded Food entries for chocolate-iced yeast doughnuts with proportional adjustment for pistachio topping. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for deep-fried doughnuts. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adapted USDA source is a close structural match and the adaptation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18247 (Doughnuts, yeast-leavened, glazed, enriched) and USDA Branded Food entries for chocolate-iced yeast doughnuts. A proportional adjustment was applied for the pistachio garnish (~5 g per 75 g donut). 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy doughnut entries and is not expected to be a significant contributor in this product. 3. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for deep-fried doughnuts (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 4. Trans fat value of 0.3 g/100 g is based on USDA Branded Food data for comparable commercial chocolate-iced yeast doughnuts; actual trans fat may vary by production batch and supplier. 5. Halal status is listed as false because Dunkin' Donuts Singapore halal certification status was not confirmed from an approved primary source at time of processing; this field should be verified against MUIS or the operator's official halal certificate. 6. The food_name has been expanded from the input 'Pistachio Chocolate' to 'Pistachio Chocolate Donut' to provide a canonical, unambiguous English food identity consistent with the product category. 7. Vitamin C is 0.0; this is confirmed zero as deep-fried wheat-based donuts with chocolate glaze contain no meaningful vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central data.
